world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Industrial island complex with visible mutant captivity infrastructure; outdoor and facility-based level design
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy forces actively patrolling and defending facilities; captive mutants shown in distress or confinement animations

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Genosha environment dominated by militaristic/authoritarian architecture; color palette emphasizing control and oppression
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Organized enemy patrols; coordinated resistance; boss-level threats as faction leadership

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Holographic/digital training environment; distinct visual separation from organic world; testing chamber aesthetic
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Holographic enemy replicas (Omega Red, Juggernaut holograms); controlled combat scenarios

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: High-tech, space-based environment; technological/magnetic visual language; distinct from organic Genosha
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Magneto's forces with organized hierarchy; Exodus as lieutenant threat; Magneto as final confrontation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Stage completion; Xavier's updated mission briefings; enemy force reduction in narrative context
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Progressive difficulty scaling; new boss encounters; multi-stage campaign structure showing advancing objectives

environmental_storytelling.example: Genosha facilities show industrial/military architecture communicating Apocalypse's oppressive control structure
Captive mutants shown imprisoned in level geometry communicates moral urgency and faction power dynamics
Facility design (platforms, obstacles) reflects militarized territory; dangerous architecture as environmental hostility
Queen Brood and Tusk encountered in Genosha; Exodus on Avalon; Magneto in space station—location matches threat hierarchy
Organic/industrial Genosha contrasts with high-tech Avalon, visually communicating shift from planetary to cosmic threat
Five X-Men controllable from start suggests established team; each character's stage shows individual mission objectives and capabilities

Danger Room encounter; scripted holographic combat; preparation function rather than real threat
Danger Room encounter; holographic visual indicator; training progression gate
Avalon environment encounter; Magneto's visible hierarchy position; mid-final confrontation difficulty
Avalon space station location; ultimate confrontation sprite; magnetic/technological attack patterns; campaign conclusion threat

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Character-specific movement sets; unique visual attack animations; individual stage routing showing specialized roles

notes: X-Men: Mutant Apocalypse uses character-differentiation and environment-specific visual language to communicate faction control and world state. Genosha represents oppressive mutant captivity under Apocalypse; Avalon represents escalated cosmic threat from Magneto. The game's visual manifestation relies on environmental architecture (industrial/oppressive vs. high-tech), enemy unit design variety (alien/Brood vs. human soldiers vs. magnetic threats), and boss encounter placement to signal narrative progression and power hierarchy. Enemy behavior patterns (patrol formations, attack coordination) visually reinforce faction organization. Health pickups and extra life collectibles provide visual resource feedback. The Danger Room's distinct aesthetic (holographic/digital) communicates training/preparation phase separate from real combat.
